Prior to 1863 the flag of the Kingdom of Cambodia was a yellow triangular pennant with a thick green border. In 1863 the Kingdom of Cambodia was a colony under the protection of France. The flag adopted was a red field with thick blue border and a white depiction of Angkor Wat, a large temple, at the centre. ## Flag during French Protectorate, 1863-1948

image by P. Mattew, adapted by M. Schmöger ca. 1863-1948 (Kingdom of Cambodia under French protection): The first Cambodian flag probably came into existence around 1863 and was used, with only variations, until 1948. Since around 1875, the Cambodian flag has featured a depiction of Angkor Wat in the centre. The current flag, with blue border stripes and a red central one (ratio 1:2:1 blue-red-blue) was adopted in 1948 after WWII, during a period of reforms and unrest in Cambodia due to French colonial rule.
